key_insight: SplineNet embeds exact spline-based CAD geometry directly into a neural network, enabling isogeometric analysis of complex shell structures with a single unified model.
engineering_relevance: This tight CAD-CAE integration can reduce model exchange overhead and support fast shell simulation for complex real-world geometries.
method: Uses watertight spline representations such as analysis-suitable unstructured T-splines.
Builds the network with Bézier extraction and Bernstein polynomial activations.
Supports both data-free energy-based training with Kirchhoff--Love shell mechanics and data-driven DeepONet-style inference.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a variety of numerical examples showing the method works well, especially for real-world complex geometries.
watch_item: The approach depends on high-quality spline/CAD representations and careful implementation of isogeometric operators, which may limit ease of adoption.
paper_title: SplineNet: An Isogeometric Deep Learning Method for Complex Shells
